docs.title: Forge User Docs (DE/EN)
docs.text: This directory contains user-facing documentation in English and German.

docs.title: Contribution
docs.text: Contribution workflow and DE/EN parity checklist: - docs/user/CONTRIBUTING.md

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Update the primary page in one locale.
  2. Update the counterpart in the other locale.
  3. Verify internal links and section parity.
  4. Submit PR with the checklist below.

docs.title: PR Checklist
docs.text:
• EN page updated
  • DE page updated (or explicitly marked pending)
  • Links checked in both locales
  • Command examples verified against current CLI help
  • Trust/Safety wording still matches current capability boundaries

docs.title: Required Locale Pairs
docs.text: Each page under docs/user/en/ must have an equivalent in docs/user/de/.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Keep user docs concise and operational.
  • Move deep implementation details to feature/issue docs and link to them.

docs.title: Home
docs.text:
EN • DE • Dark Forge - Your AI Repo Workbench

With control, not magic. Forge helps you query, review, test, and improve repositories with explicit steps, visible evidence, and auditable outputs.

Get Started See Commands

docs.title: Why Forge
docs.text:
Explicit Modes

Mode behavior stays understandable: query, review, describe, test.

Human-Auditable

Outputs are grounded in paths, matches, diagnostics, and traceable decisions.

Composable Foundations

Advanced workflows are built from inspectable core foundations.

Useful by Default

Strong defaults, optional configuration, and local-first repository workflows.

docs.title: Quick CLI Example
docs.text:
forge query "Where is session TTL configured?"
forge review src/checkout.py --focus reliability
forge describe core/workspace_foundation.py

docs.title: Choose your entry point — based on time and goal
docs.text:
Quick Start 
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
python -m pip install -e .
forge init --non-interactive --template typo3-v14
forge query "Where is the runtime settings resolver implemented?"
Installation and Setup
  • Guided Setup
  • Installation
  • LLM Setup
Documentation

Navigate user docs, developer foundations, and the full open-source repository.

  • User Documentation
  • Developer Documentation
  • GitHub Repository

docs.title: Trust, Safety, Openness
docs.text:
• Developer Documentation: Foundations, contracts, and architecture notes live in docs/developer/ in the repository.
  • Trust & Safety: Rules, safeguards, and boundaries are explicitly documented.
  • Logging & Limits: Runtime limits (read/write scope) and diagnostics transparency are documented in Runtime Settings & Sessions.
  • LLM Providers & Local LLM: Forge supports OpenAI-compatible endpoints, including OpenAI, LiteLLM, and vLLM setups, documented in LLM Setup.
  • Open Source (MIT): Forge is available under the MIT license (LICENSE in the repository).

docs.title: Core Commands
docs.text: This page explains the main Forge commands, their typical parameters, and when to use them.

docs.title: Global Parameters (apply to all commands)
docs.text:
• --repo-root <path>: start path for repo detection (.forge/ marker lookup).
  • --env-file <path>: explicit .env file.
  • --output-format text|json: human text or machine-readable contract output.
  • --view compact|standard|full: text detail level.
  • --details: shorthand for --view full.
  • --llm-mode auto|off|force: LLM usage policy.
  • --llm-provider <id>, --llm-model <id>, --llm-base-url <url>, --llm-timeout-s <n>
  • --llm-output-language <lang|auto>
  • --query-input-mode planner|exact: query interpretation mode.

Many capability commands also accept an optional profile prefix in positional input: - simple - standard - detailed

Example:

forge --output-format json query detailed "Where is enrich_detailed_context defined?"

docs.title: Query
docs.text:
forge query [--framework-profile <id>] <question>

Use query to locate files, symbols, and evidence snippets for concrete repo questions.

Important parameters: - --framework-profile <id>: load optional framework profile from .forge/frameworks.toml. - global: --query-input-mode planner|exact, --llm-*, output/view parameters.

Example:

forge query --query-input-mode exact "def load_framework_graph_references"

docs.title: Ask (+ Presets)
docs.text:
• forge ask <question>
  • forge ask:repo <question>
  • forge ask:docs <question>
  • forge ask:latest <question>

Use ask for broader Q/A. ask:* presets control source strategy.

Important parameters: - --framework-profile / --profile (alias) - --guided (reserved staged mode) - global: --llm-*, output/view parameters

Example:

forge ask:docs "Which config key controls query source scope?"

docs.title: Explain (+ Facet Aliases)
docs.text:
• forge explain <target>
  • aliases: forge explain:dependencies <target>, explain:settings, explain:outputs, ...

Use explain for deeper, target-focused analysis.

Important parameters: - --focus overview|symbols|dependencies|resources|uses|settings|defaults|llm|outputs - --direction out|in (dependency-like facets) - --source-scope repo_only|framework_only|all - --from-run <id> + --confirm-transition

Example:

forge explain --focus dependencies --direction out core/graph_cache.py

docs.title: Review
docs.text:
forge review <target>

Use review for deterministic findings based on repository evidence.

Important parameters: - --from-run <id> - --confirm-transition

docs.title: Describe
docs.text:
forge describe [target]

Use describe for repo/module orientation and summaries.

Important parameters: - --from-run <id> - --confirm-transition

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
forge test <target>

Use test to draft test plans and cases from existing code context.

Important parameters: - --from-run <id> - --confirm-transition

docs.title: Doctor and Config
docs.text:
• forge doctor [--check-llm-endpoint]
  • forge config validate (alias path)

Use for setup/config diagnostics.

docs.title: Runs
docs.text:
forge runs ...

Common usage: - forge runs list - forge runs last - forge runs <id> show [compact|standard|full] - forge runs <id> rerun - forge runs prune [--keep-last N] [--older-than-days D] [--dry-run]

docs.title: Logs
docs.text:
forge logs ...

Common usage: - forge logs tail [count] - forge logs run <run_id> - forge logs show <event_id> - forge logs stats

Filter parameters: - --run-id, --capability, --step-type, --status - --since, --until, --provider, --model

docs.title: Init and Index
docs.text:
• forge init [--template ...] [--non-interactive] [--force] [--dry-run] ...
  • forge index

Use init to bootstrap .forge config/artifacts and index to build repo metadata.

docs.title: Session / Set / Get
docs.text:
• forge session new|use|list|show|clear-context|end ...
  • forge set [--scope session|repo|user] <key> <value>
  • forge get [--scope ...] [--resolved] [--source] [key]

These commands control runtime settings and session behavior. See: runtime-settings-and-sessions.md.

docs.title: Does Forge edit my code by default?
docs.text: No. Analysis modes are read-only for source code.

docs.title: Can I use a local model?
docs.text: Yes, via any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

docs.title: Why JSON output?
docs.text: JSON exposes deterministic contracts for automation and auditing.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
python -m pip install -e .
forge init --non-interactive --template balanced
forge query "Where is load_framework_graph_references defined?"

docs.title: First checks
docs.text:
• forge doctor
  • forge --output-format json query "Where is X defined?"

docs.title: Development install
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install -e .

docs.title: Workstation install with pipx
docs.text:
python3 -m pip install --user pipx
python3 -m pipx ensurepath
pipx install forge-repo-tool

forge-repo-tool is the package name, forge is the CLI command.

docs.title: LLM Setup
docs.text: Forge supports OpenAI-compatible endpoints, including self-hosted/local setups.

docs.title: Example config
docs.text:
[llm]
provider = "openai_compatible"
base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1"
model = "local/devstral-small-24b"

docs.title: Deterministic fallback
docs.text: forge --llm-mode off query "Where is the cache built?"

docs.title: Find a function definition
docs.text: forge query "Where is log_llm_event defined?"

docs.title: Explain dependencies of one file
docs.text: forge explain:dependencies core/graph_cache.py

docs.title: Focus one quality gate
docs.text: PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/run_quality_gates.py --only gate_runtime_settings_set_get

docs.title: Runtime Settings & Sessions
docs.text: This page explains runtime scopes, setting precedence, and session lifecycle commands.

docs.title: Runtime Scopes
docs.text:
Forge runtime settings can come from:

  • session scope (active named session)
  • repo scope (.forge/runtime.toml)
  • user scope (user runtime config)
  • config files (.forge/config.toml, .forge/config.local.toml)
  • defaults

docs.title: Effective Precedence (high -> low)
docs.text:
1. explicit CLI flags
  2. session scope
  3. repo scope
  4. user scope
  5. config TOML values
  6. defaults

Use forge get --source to inspect where a value came from.

docs.title: forge set
docs.text:
forge set [--scope session|repo|user] <key> <value>

Examples:

forge set --scope session output.view full
forge set --scope repo llm.mode off
forge set --scope user access.web off

Notes: - default scope is session - aliases are supported (for example output, llm, execution, access web|write)

docs.title: forge get
docs.text:
forge get [--scope session|repo|user] [--resolved] [--source] [key]

Examples:

forge get
forge get llm.mode --source
forge get --scope repo output
forge get --resolved --source

Behavior: - without --scope, returns resolved/effective view - --scope reads raw values from one scope - --resolved forces effective merge view - --source includes per-key provenance

docs.title: forge session
docs.text:
forge session [--ttl-minutes N] [--revive] <command>

Commands: - new <name> - use <name> - list - show [name] - clear-context [name] - end [name]

Examples:

forge session new work --ttl-minutes 180
forge session use work
forge session list
forge session clear-context work
forge session end work

docs.title: 1) Per-repo baseline + session override
docs.text:
forge set --scope repo llm.mode auto
forge set --scope repo output.format text
forge session new triage --ttl-minutes 120
forge set --scope session output.view full
forge get --resolved --source

docs.title: 2) Strict local policy
docs.text:
forge set --scope user access.web off
forge set --scope user access.write off
forge get access --resolved --source

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• If a value does not behave as expected, run:
  • forge get <key> --resolved --source
  • If session values are stale/expired, switch with:
  • forge session use <name> --revive

docs.title: No initialized Forge repository found
docs.text:
Run:

forge init --non-interactive --template balanced

docs.title: LLM endpoint errors
docs.text:
• check llm.base_url
  • run forge doctor --check-llm-endpoint

docs.title: Unexpected query ranking
docs.text: • inspect JSON output: sections.likely_locations, sections.evidence, sections.uncertainty

docs.title: Trust & Safety
docs.text: Forge favors explicit capability boundaries over hidden automation.

docs.title: Read scope
docs.text:
• repository files
  • .forge artifacts (index.json, graph.json, runs/logs)
  • optional framework refs/docs
  • optional web retrieval in ask presets (when enabled)

docs.title: Write scope
docs.text:
• current writes are limited to Forge-owned artifacts/settings/session state
  • current analysis modes do not modify source code
  • future code-writing modes must define explicit target boundaries

docs.title: Auditability
docs.text:
• machine-readable output contracts (--output-format json)
  • explicit uncertainty/fallback sections
  • run history and logs are inspectable

docs.title: Home
docs.text:
EN • DE • Dark Forge - Your AI Repo Workbench

Mit Kontrolle statt Magie. Forge hilft beim Verstehen, Prüfen und Verbessern von Repositories mit expliziten Schritten, sichtbarer Evidenz und nachvollziehbaren Ergebnissen.

Jetzt starten Befehle ansehen

docs.title: Warum Forge
docs.text:
Explizite Modi

Das Verhalten bleibt verständlich: query, review, describe, test.

Menschlich prüfbar

Ausgaben sind an Pfade, Treffer, Diagnostics und klare Entscheidungen gebunden.

Komponierbare Foundations

Fortgeschrittene Workflows bauen auf inspizierbaren Kernbausteinen auf.

Standardmäßig nützlich

Starke Defaults, optionale Konfiguration und lokaler Repo-First-Fokus.

docs.title: Kurzes CLI-Beispiel
docs.text:
forge query "Wo wird Session TTL konfiguriert?"
forge review src/checkout.py --focus reliability
forge describe core/workspace_foundation.py

docs.title: Wähle deinen Einstieg – je nach Zeit und Ziel
docs.text:
Quick Start 
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
python -m pip install -e .
forge init --non-interactive --template typo3-v14
forge query "Wo ist der Runtime-Settings-Resolver implementiert?"
Installation und Einrichtung
  • Guided Setup
  • Installation
  • LLM-Setup
Documentation

Zugriff auf User-Doku, Developer-Foundations und das komplette Open-Source-Repository.

  • User-Dokumentation
  • Developer-Dokumentation
  • GitHub-Repository

docs.title: Trust, Safety, Offenheit
docs.text:
• Developer-Dokumentation: Foundations, Verträge und Architekturhinweise liegen in docs/developer/ im Repository.
  • Trust & Safety: Regeln, Schutzmechanismen und Grenzen sind explizit dokumentiert.
  • Logging & Limits: Laufzeitgrenzen (Read/Write-Scope) und Diagnose-Transparenz sind in Runtime-Settings & Sessions dokumentiert.
  • LLM-Provider & Local LLM: Forge unterstuetzt OpenAI-kompatible Endpunkte, inklusive OpenAI, LiteLLM und vLLM, beschrieben in LLM-Setup.
  • Open Source (MIT): Forge ist unter der MIT-Lizenz verfügbar (LICENSE im Repository).

docs.title: Kernbefehle
docs.text: Diese Seite erklaert die zentralen Forge-Befehle, typische Parameter und Einsatzfaelle.

docs.title: Allgemeine Parameter (gelten fuer alle Befehle)
docs.text:
• --repo-root <path>: Startpfad fuer Repo-Erkennung (.forge/-Marker).
  • --env-file <path>: explizite .env-Datei.
  • --output-format text|json: Textausgabe oder maschinenlesbarer Contract.
  • --view compact|standard|full: Detailgrad der Textausgabe.
  • --details: Kurzform fuer --view full.
  • --llm-mode auto|off|force: LLM-Nutzungspolitik.
  • --llm-provider <id>, --llm-model <id>, --llm-base-url <url>, --llm-timeout-s <n>
  • --llm-output-language <lang|auto>
  • --query-input-mode planner|exact: Query-Interpretationsmodus.

Viele Capability-Befehle akzeptieren zusaetzlich ein Profil-Praefix im Payload: - simple - standard - detailed

Beispiel:

forge --output-format json query detailed "Wo ist enrich_detailed_context definiert?"

docs.title: Query
docs.text:
forge query [--framework-profile <id>] <frage>

query findet Dateien, Symbole und Evidenzstellen fuer konkrete Repo-Fragen.

Wichtige Parameter: - --framework-profile <id>: optionales Framework-Profil aus .forge/frameworks.toml. - global: --query-input-mode planner|exact, --llm-*, output/view-Parameter.

Beispiel:

forge query --query-input-mode exact "def load_framework_graph_references"

docs.title: Ask (+ Presets)
docs.text:
• forge ask <frage>
  • forge ask:repo <frage>
  • forge ask:docs <frage>
  • forge ask:latest <frage>

ask ist fuer breitere Q/A. ask:*-Presets steuern die Quellenstrategie.

Wichtige Parameter: - --framework-profile / --profile (Alias) - --guided (staged/reserviert) - global: --llm-*, output/view-Parameter

Beispiel:

forge ask:docs "Welcher config key steuert query source scope?"

docs.title: Explain (+ Facet-Aliase)
docs.text:
• forge explain <target>
  • Aliase: forge explain:dependencies <target>, explain:settings, explain:outputs, ...

explain liefert tiefe, zielgerichtete Analyse.

Wichtige Parameter: - --focus overview|symbols|dependencies|resources|uses|settings|defaults|llm|outputs - --direction out|in (fuer dependency-aehnliche Facets) - --source-scope repo_only|framework_only|all - --from-run <id> + --confirm-transition

Beispiel:

forge explain --focus dependencies --direction out core/graph_cache.py

docs.title: Review
docs.text:
forge review <target>

review liefert deterministische Findings auf Basis von Repo-Evidenz.

Wichtige Parameter: - --from-run <id> - --confirm-transition

docs.title: Describe
docs.text:
forge describe [target]

describe ist fuer Repo-/Modul-Ueberblick und Zusammenfassungen.

Wichtige Parameter: - --from-run <id> - --confirm-transition

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
forge test <target>

test entwirft Testplaene/-faelle aus realem Code-Kontext.

Wichtige Parameter: - --from-run <id> - --confirm-transition

docs.title: Doctor und Config
docs.text:
• forge doctor [--check-llm-endpoint]
  • forge config validate (Alias-Pfad)

Fuer Setup-/Konfigurationsdiagnostik.

docs.title: Runs
docs.text:
forge runs ...

Typische Nutzung: - forge runs list - forge runs last - forge runs <id> show [compact|standard|full] - forge runs <id> rerun - forge runs prune [--keep-last N] [--older-than-days D] [--dry-run]

docs.title: Logs
docs.text:
forge logs ...

Typische Nutzung: - forge logs tail [count] - forge logs run <run_id> - forge logs show <event_id> - forge logs stats

Filter-Parameter: - --run-id, --capability, --step-type, --status - --since, --until, --provider, --model

docs.title: Init und Index
docs.text:
• forge init [--template ...] [--non-interactive] [--force] [--dry-run] ...
  • forge index

init bootstrapped .forge-Konfiguration/Artefakte, index baut Repo-Metadaten.

docs.title: Session / Set / Get
docs.text:
• forge session new|use|list|show|clear-context|end ...
  • forge set [--scope session|repo|user] <key> <value>
  • forge get [--scope ...] [--resolved] [--source] [key]

Diese Befehle steuern Runtime-Settings und Session-Verhalten. Siehe: runtime-settings-and-sessions.md.

docs.title: Bearbeitet Forge meinen Code standardmaessig?
docs.text: Nein. Analysemodi sind fuer Source-Code read-only.

docs.title: Kann ich ein lokales Modell nutzen?
docs.text: Ja, ueber jeden OpenAI-kompatiblen Endpunkt.

docs.title: Warum JSON-Ausgabe?
docs.text: JSON macht Contracts fuer Automatisierung und Auditierung explizit.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
python -m pip install -e .
forge init --non-interactive --template balanced
forge query "Wo ist load_framework_graph_references definiert?"

docs.title: Erste Checks
docs.text:
• forge doctor
  • forge --output-format json query "Wo ist X definiert?"

docs.title: Entwicklungsinstallation
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install -e .

docs.title: Workstation-Installation mit pipx
docs.text:
python3 -m pip install --user pipx
python3 -m pipx ensurepath
pipx install forge-repo-tool

forge-repo-tool ist der Paketname, forge ist der CLI-Befehl.

docs.title: LLM-Setup
docs.text: Forge unterstuetzt OpenAI-kompatible Endpunkte, auch self-hosted/lokal.

docs.title: Beispielkonfiguration
docs.text:
[llm]
provider = "openai_compatible"
base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1"
model = "local/devstral-small-24b"

docs.title: Deterministischer Fallback
docs.text: forge --llm-mode off query "Wo wird der Cache gebaut?"

docs.title: Funktionsdefinition finden
docs.text: forge query "Wo ist log_llm_event definiert?"

docs.title: Abhaengigkeiten einer Datei erklaeren
docs.text: forge explain:dependencies core/graph_cache.py

docs.title: Einzelnes Quality Gate ausfuehren
docs.text: PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/run_quality_gates.py --only gate_runtime_settings_set_get

docs.title: Runtime-Settings & Sessions
docs.text: Diese Seite erklaert Scopes, Prioritaeten und Session-Lifecycle-Befehle.

docs.title: Runtime-Scopes
docs.text:
Forge-Runtime-Settings koennen aus folgenden Quellen kommen:

  • session-Scope (aktive benannte Session)
  • repo-Scope (.forge/runtime.toml)
  • user-Scope (user runtime config)
  • config-Dateien (.forge/config.toml, .forge/config.local.toml)
  • Defaults

docs.title: Effektive Prioritaet (hoch -> niedrig)
docs.text:
1. explizite CLI-Flags
  2. session scope
  3. repo scope
  4. user scope
  5. config TOML values
  6. defaults

Mit forge get --source kannst du die Herkunft jedes Werts sehen.

docs.title: forge set
docs.text:
forge set [--scope session|repo|user] <key> <value>

Beispiele:

forge set --scope session output.view full
forge set --scope repo llm.mode off
forge set --scope user access.web off

Hinweise: - Default-Scope ist session - Aliase werden unterstuetzt (z. B. output, llm, execution, access web|write)

docs.title: forge get
docs.text:
forge get [--scope session|repo|user] [--resolved] [--source] [key]

Beispiele:

forge get
forge get llm.mode --source
forge get --scope repo output
forge get --resolved --source

Verhalten: - ohne --scope: aufgeloeste/effektive Sicht - --scope: rohe Werte aus genau einem Scope - --resolved: erzwingt die effektive Merge-Sicht - --source: fuegt pro Key die Herkunft hinzu

docs.title: forge session
docs.text:
forge session [--ttl-minutes N] [--revive] <command>

Kommandos: - new <name> - use <name> - list - show [name] - clear-context [name] - end [name]

Beispiele:

forge session new work --ttl-minutes 180
forge session use work
forge session list
forge session clear-context work
forge session end work

docs.title: 1) Repo-Basis + Session-Override
docs.text:
forge set --scope repo llm.mode auto
forge set --scope repo output.format text
forge session new triage --ttl-minutes 120
forge set --scope session output.view full
forge get --resolved --source

docs.title: 2) Strikte lokale Policy
docs.text:
forge set --scope user access.web off
forge set --scope user access.write off
forge get access --resolved --source

docs.title: Fehlerbehebung
docs.text:
• Wenn ein Wert unerwartet wirkt:
  • forge get <key> --resolved --source
  • Wenn Session-Werte veraltet/abgelaufen sind:
  • forge session use <name> --revive

docs.title: No initialized Forge repository found
docs.text:
Ausfuehren:

forge init --non-interactive --template balanced

docs.title: LLM-Endpunktfehler
docs.text:
• llm.base_url pruefen
  • forge doctor --check-llm-endpoint ausfuehren

docs.title: Unerwartetes Query-Ranking
docs.text: • JSON-Ausgabe pruefen: sections.likely_locations, sections.evidence, sections.uncertainty

docs.title: Trust & Safety
docs.text: Forge setzt auf explizite Capability-Grenzen statt versteckter Automatisierung.

docs.title: Lese-Scope
docs.text:
• Repository-Dateien
  • .forge-Artefakte (index.json, graph.json, runs/logs)
  • optionale Framework-Refs/-Docs
  • optionale Web-Retrieval-Pfade in Ask-Presets (wenn aktiviert)

docs.title: Write-Scope
docs.text:
• aktuelle Schreibvorgaenge sind auf Forge-Artefakte/Settings/Session-State begrenzt
  • aktuelle Analysemodi aendern keinen Source-Code
  • zukuenftige Code-Write-Modi muessen explizite Zielgrenzen definieren

docs.title: Auditierbarkeit
docs.text:
• maschinenlesbare Output-Contracts (--output-format json)
  • explizite Unsicherheits-/Fallback-Sektionen
  • Run-Historie und Logs sind einsehbar
